summary: This is a brief informational post explaining the feed options available for Martin Fowler's website. He offers an updates feed for site changes and a bliki feed that combines blog posts with site updates. The post clarifies that despite using 'RSS Feed' terminology, the feeds are actually Atom format. He also mentions the ThoughtBlogs aggregated feed and notes that older RSS 0.91 feeds are deprecated.
key_points: Two main feeds available: updates-only and bliki (which includes both blog posts and updates)
Feeds use Atom format despite common RSS terminology
ThoughtBlogs feed syndicates content from multiple ThoughtWorks bloggers including Fowler
Legacy RSS 0.91 feeds are deprecated but still functional
money_quotes: Although I've used the term RSS Feed here, they are actually Atom feeds.
This is the one to pick if you want to know about site updates but don't care to read my blog.
I will remove them if I ever have to put any effort to keep them running.
key_insight: A practical housekeeping post explaining subscription options, notable mainly for its pragmatic approach to legacy format deprecation.
